title: I Am Becoming Her: The New Her
subtitle: An affirmation for the identity transition of motherhood
excerpt: An affirmation for women in the middle of matrescence: the identity rewrite of becoming a mother. For use when the old self has changed and the new self has not yet fully formed.

practice.whenToUse: Use in the early period of motherhood when the identity is in transition: when you no longer know quite who you are but can feel something new forming.

content.text: An affirmation for the middle of matrescence: the long transition between the person you were before and the person you are becoming. The middle is disorienting. This statement names where you are without requiring the new self to be fully formed yet.

Becoming a mother changes who you are. It changes what you do and what your days look like. It changes something deeper: who you are. The former self does not simply continue with a baby added to it. She changes. Parts of her disappear. New parts emerge. The process takes longer than anyone says, and the middle of it does not feel stable.
I am becoming her. The new her.

Say it in the morning, standing in the bathroom or while the kettle boils. The affirmation works before the new her is visible. The recognition that a becoming is in progress is enough. Say it on days when you feel the grief of the person you were. Say it on days when you feel something new and unfamiliar that is also yours.
